summary: One-line GX header fix: `set_x2(GXBool value)` in `src/dolphin/gx/__GXInit.h` was changed from `static` to `static inline`. The PR body says this "Fixes context," suggesting the helper needed to inline at call sites rather than emit or affect a standalone local function/context.
what_changed: `src/dolphin/gx/__GXInit.h` changed the declaration/definition of `set_x2` from `static void set_x2(GXBool value)` to `static inline void set_x2(GXBool value)`.
The function body remained unchanged: it assigns `value` into `__GXContexts.main->x0.u16[1]`.
No call sites or behavior were changed in the diff excerpt; this was a linkage/inlining/codegen-context adjustment.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal: only the storage/inlining qualifier changed, reducing risk in a low-level Dolphin GX header.
Used `static inline` for a small header-defined helper, which is appropriate when the helper is meant to be expanded into includers instead of producing an out-of-line function body.
Preserved the existing function name, signature, and assignment expression, isolating the change to compiler code generation/context behavior.
decomp_lessons: Header-defined helper functions in matching code may need explicit `inline` to reproduce original codegen/context behavior.
When a tiny helper only writes a field and is included from a private system header, changing `static` to `static inline` can be a precise matching fix without altering semantics.
For decompilation, qualifiers on helper definitions are part of the match surface; even a one-token header change can affect whether surrounding functions match.
naming_conventions: `set_x2` is a lowercase helper name with an offset-like field suffix, matching its direct write into `x0.u16[1]`.
`__GXInit.h` and `__GXContexts` follow Dolphin/GX internal naming with double-underscore-prefixed private globals/headers.
`GXBool` is retained as the parameter type rather than using a generic integer or bool type.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `static inline` for very small private header helpers when an out-of-line `static` function changes context or prevents a match.
Prefer qualifier-only edits when the body already expresses the correct operation and only compiler treatment needs adjustment.
For field-setting helpers that compile into callers, preserve the exact field access expression while tuning inline behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
One review occurred according to the metadata, but no review body or comments were available to extract intent beyond the PR body statement: "Fixes context."
follow_up_queries: Find other header-defined GX helper functions that are `static` but may need `static inline` for matching.
Search for `set_x2` call sites to identify which function context this PR fixed.
Compare generated code before and after PR #737 to document the exact context/matching difference.
